card_name: The Masked Saiyan // Bardock, Unbound by Darkness (2018 Big Card Pack)
ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly names both identities: 'The Masked Saiyan' (controlled form) and 'Bardock, Unbound by Darkness' (freed form). The double-face name structure is the canonical presentation of Bardock's Xenoverse/Heroes arc. Dark armor + white mask + black ki is the canonical Masked Saiyan visual register. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: SD3-01 is a promotional card from the DBS Card Game's 2018 Big Card Pack (Starter Deck 3). The double-faced card name 'The Masked Saiyan // Bardock, Unbound by Darkness' encodes both sides of the arc: Bardock under Towa's mask-control, and Bardock freed when the mask is broken. [dbs-cardgame.com official card list, via WebSearch]

The Masked Saiyan's identity was first revealed in Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2's story campaign: Bardock was pulled from the moment before his death by Frieza's Supernova and brainwashed by Towa using Demon Realm technology. The mask provided a constant supply of Dark Ki keeping him under her control. The mask is broken when the Future Warrior defeats him in battle — purging the Dark Ki and freeing Bardock. This is the canonical mask-reveal moment across Xenoverse 2 and Dragon Ball Heroes. [Dragon Ball Wiki: The Masked Saiyan Saga; Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 Wiki: Masked Saiyan, via WebSearch]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: The Masked Saiyan Saga
Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 Wiki: Masked Saiyan

trivia_bullets.text: Bardock as the Masked Saiyan originates in the Dragon Ball Heroes arcade game (Japan) and was then adapted into Xenoverse 2 as story content. The card art depicts the masked form — dark armor, white mask, black/dark-red ki — not the unmasked reveal. This is the controlled-identity face of the double-card, framing the art as the moment of maximum suppression before the break. [Dragon Ball Wiki: The Masked Saiyan Saga; Masked Saiyan — Videogaming Wiki, via WebSearch]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: The Masked Saiyan Saga

trivia_bullets.text: The Masked Saiyan arc functions as a retroactive tragedy for Bardock — a character originally known only from the 1990 TV special as Goku's father who died predicting Frieza's betrayal. Xenoverse 2 extends his story: he survives (sort of), is enslaved, is freed, and then fights alongside the Time Patrol. The mask-concealment / identity-restoration arc is the structural core of his post-Frieza narrative. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Bardock; Videogaming Wiki: Masked Saiyan, via WebSearch]

related_cards.name: Towa
related_cards.note: The Demon Realm scientist who built the mask and enslaved Bardock

related_cards.name: Future Warrior
related_cards.note: The player character who breaks the mask in Xenoverse 2
